summary: Tested AI Itinerary Generator feature and backend refactoring regression. Backend: All 25 tests passed (100%). AI endpoint POST /api/ai/itinerary generates structured JSON with days, activities, meals, transfers, tips. Frontend: AI Generate Itinerary button visible in TripBuilder, clicking generates itinerary and shows modal with day cards containing activities, meals, transfers, and tips. Backend refactoring regression passed - all existing endpoints (Auth, Hotels, Insurance, Proposals, Activities, Transfers, Cities, Airports) work correctly after models extracted to schemas.py.

ai_itinerary_endpoint.details: Generates structured JSON with days array containing activities, meals, transfers, tips. Tested single city (Tbilisi 3 nights), multi-city (Tbilisi+Baku), and without optional interests field - all working.

ai_itinerary_button_visibility.details: AI Generate Itinerary button visible next to 'Your Itinerary' heading in TripBuilder. Uses data-testid='ai-generate-itinerary-btn'. Purple gradient button with compass icon.

ai_itinerary_modal.details: Clicking AI button opens modal (data-testid='ai-itinerary-modal') showing day cards with: Activities (name, time, duration, description), Meals (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner suggestions), Transfers, Tips. Has Close and Regenerate buttons.

auth_endpoints_regression.details: All auth endpoints working after models extracted to schemas.py

hotels_crud_regression.details: Hotels CRUD working, city filter working, sorting by recommended working

insurance_endpoints_regression.details: Insurance settings CRUD working, country filter working

activities_crud_regression.details: Activities endpoints working with city filter

transfers_crud_regression.details: Transfers endpoints working with city filter

critical_code_review_comments: AI itinerary endpoint correctly uses Gemini AI via emergentintegrations.llm.chat with proper JSON parsing
Models are correctly imported from models/schemas.py in server.py line 79-86
Route modules in /app/backend/routes/ are created but NOT imported in server.py - routes are still defined inline in server.py
TripBuilder.jsx correctly implements AI button at lines 1107-1125 with data-testid='ai-generate-itinerary-btn'
AI endpoint has 30 second timeout configured for Gemini API calls

success_rate.frontend: 100% (AI itinerary button, modal, and content verified)

seed_data_creation: Created test proposals during testing, automatically cleaned up
context_for_next_testing_agent: AI Itinerary Generator fully implemented and tested. Backend uses Gemini AI via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. Frontend button data-testid: ai-generate-itinerary-btn, modal data-testid: ai-itinerary-modal. AI generation takes 15-25 seconds. Backend refactoring created models/schemas.py, db.py, and routes/ folder but routes are still inline in server.py (only models are imported from schemas.py). All existing CRUD endpoints continue to work after refactoring.
